rulings.Case Title: CHARLES PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 30, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Anning-Johnson Company¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar as a duplicate entry. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 30, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Anning-Johnson Company¿s motion for summary judgment: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 30, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: IVAN MERL BARNEY VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 30, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: IVAN MERL BARNEY VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 30, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Deere & Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Barney was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
